article_title: ServiceNow Patches an Unauthenticated API Endpoint That Let Customer Instance Tables Be Queried Without Credentials
summary: Substantively accurate and well-sourced; two quote-boundary issues (paraphrase wrapped in quote marks, two adjacent quoted fragments recomposed) are publishable with a corrections trail.

findings.message: Cory Michal quote includes a CSO Online paraphrase inside the quotation marks.
findings.details: The article quotes Michal as 'An unauthenticated, internet-facing ServiceNow API endpoint that could be accessed without authentication when certain conditions were present.' In the CSO Online snapshot, only 'An unauthenticated, internet-facing ServiceNow API endpoint' is inside quotation marks; the clause 'that could be accessed without authentication when certain conditions were present' is CSO's own narration outside the quote. Subordinate body claim, fact accurate — recoverable via corrections note.

findings.message: ServiceNow attribution quote recomposes two adjacent quoted fragments into one continuous quote.
findings.details: The article renders ServiceNow as saying '...conducting their own research. However, our investigation is ongoing,'. CSO Online quotes two separate fragments: 'We have reason to believe the observed activity can be attributed to security researchers or customers conducting their own research,' and 'Our investigation is ongoing, however, and subject to additional validation.' Every quoted word appears verbatim in the source and the meaning is preserved, but the merge and the relocation of 'however' is an imprecise reconstruction. Flagged as a clarification.

content_preview.title: ServiceNow Patches an Unauthenticated API Endpoint That Let Customer Instance Tables Be Queried Without Credentials
content_preview.summary: ServiceNow fixed a REST API endpoint that shipped without authentication, after confirming a subset of customer instances were queried. It has not assigned a CVE.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

ServiceNow has disclosed a security incident and deployed a fix after a REST API endpoint on its enterprise platform was found to be reachable without authentication. According to [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/servicenow-discloses-security-incident-exposing-customer-data/), the vulnerable endpoint was `/api/now/related_list_edit/create`, and the security update set its `requires_authentication` flag to `true` after it had previously accepted unaut...

editor_notes.content_quality: Disciplined News-format piece structured as Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know. Every sentence carries an inline attribution to one of the two cited outlets. Technical specifics (endpoint path, requires_authentication flag, KB bulletin numbers, IP IoC) are precise and each maps to a snapshot. 565 words, appropriate for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read both gzipped snapshots from disk (no re-fetch). source-0.html.gz (BleepingComputer, 'ServiceNow discloses security incident exposing customer data', Lawrence Abrams, June 9 2026 with 6/10 update) — confirms the /api/now/related_list_edit/create endpoint, requires_authentication false->true, the 'could allow an unauthenticated user...than intended' quote (verbatim), the IP 51.159.98.241 IoC (this outlet ALONE — article cites it to BleepingComputer alone, no compound citation), the April 22 2026 bug bounty submission, the June 5 patch, the 'began days earlier' phrasing (verbatim, no fabricated June 2-3 window), the Australia release scope, the data-types list, and the security-researcher attribution. source-1.html.gz (CSO Online, Shweta Sharma, Jun 11 2026) — confirms June 5 update, KB3067321 (hosted) and KB3067372 (self-hosted) verbatim, requires_authentication = false on a Scripted REST resource, the 'subset of customer instances were queried successfully' quote (verbatim), the 90-day log-review advice, and the ServiceNow attribution language. Both allowlisted outlets independently carry the central thesis.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No fabricated CVE — the article correctly and repeatedly states ServiceNow has NOT assigned a CVE, matching BleepingComputer's 'still evaluating whether it will publish a CVE' and CSO's KB-bulletin framing (Rule 2 satisfied). The unconfirmed early-June activity window is correctly hedged as 'days earlier' exactly as BleepingComputer phrased it; no non-allowlisted specifics leaked in. Two quote-boundary defects identified (see findings): a CSO paraphrase pulled inside Michal's quote marks, and a recomposition of two adjacent ServiceNow quoted fragments. Both are recoverable.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral throughout. No sensationalism, no editorializing, no AI self-reference. Explicitly separates confirmed facts from open questions.
editor_notes.originality: Not a duplicate. Prior ServiceNow articles in the archive cover AI-agent product launches (Otto/Action Fabric at Knowledge 2026), unrelated to this security incident. New topic.
concerns: One Cory Michal quote wraps a CSO Online paraphrase inside the quotation marks (only the opening clause was actually quoted by CSO).
The ServiceNow attribution quote merges two separate adjacent quoted fragments and relocates the word 'however'; every word is verbatim but the reconstruction is imprecise.
recommendations: Filed a corrections record documenting both quote-boundary issues so readers see the precise source boundaries alongside the published article.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, well-attributed, original News piece carried by two allowlisted outlets with no fabricated CVE and correct hedging of the unconfirmed timeline. The only defects are two minor quote-boundary imprecisions, each honestly summarizable in a corrections note. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
